title: fagus sylvatica subsp. moesiaca
intro: Fagus sylvatica subsp. moesiaca, also known as the Balkan beech, is a subspecies of European beech that is native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Fagus sylvatica subsp. moesiaca can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support respiratory health and boost the immune system.
